ground_truth.title: 51 Must-Have Electrician Tools for 2025 - FieldEdge
ground_truth.author: Tom Talbot
ground_truth.main_content: Electricians like the ones in your business rely on more than just skills, and having the right electrician tools can make or break a project. The right electrician tools ensure your team can work safely, efficiently and profitably. In this guide, we've organized 51 must-have electrician tools for 2025 into categories so you can quickly see what belongs in your kit. Plus, we'll share one bonus tool that doesn't fit in your toolbox—but can transform your electrical business. The tools are organized into these categories: Basic Hand Tools (Multimeter, Wire Strippers, Needle-Nose Pliers, Lineman's Pliers, Screwdrivers, Utility Knife, Headlamp, Measuring Tape, Cable Cutters, Portable Work Light), Testing and Measuring Tools (Circuit Tester, Non-Contact Voltage Tester, Outlet Tester, Digital Clamp Meter, Thermal Imaging Camera, Oscilloscope, Electrical Tester with FieldSense, Insulation Tester/Megohmmeter), Safety Gear and PPE (Insulated Gloves, Arc Flash PPE, Safety Lockout/Tagout Kit, Voltage Detector Pen), Installation and Wire Management (Wire Nuts and Connectors, Conduit Bender, Stud Finder, Wire Markers and Labels, Wire Pulling Lubricant, Wire Pulling System, Wire Pulling Grips, Fish Tape, Electrical Tape, Cable Fault Locator, Conduit Reamer, Digital Caliper, Cable Pulling Winch), Power and Specialty Tools (Drill and Bits, Pipe Threading Machine, Cable Tray Cutter, Torque Wrench, Load Bank Tester), Digital Tools for the Modern Electrician (Electrical Panel Schedule Software, Cable Fault Finder, Fiber Optic Test Equipment, Power Quality Analyzer). The bonus tool is field service software that saves hours of paperwork by streamlining scheduling and invoicing.
with: Electricians like the ones in your business rely on more than just skills, and having the right electrician tools can make or break a project.
In this guide, we've organized 51 must-have electrician tools for 2025 into categories so you can quickly see what belongs in your kit.
A multimeter is the backbone of every electrician's toolkit. It measures voltage, current and resistance, allowing you to troubleshoot electrical problems accurately.
Personal safety should always be a top priority for electricians. Also, it's a requirement. Insulated gloves provide protection against electric shock while working with live wires.
Just like the right hand tools save you time on the jobsite, the best electrician software saves you hours of paperwork by streamlining scheduling and invoicing.
